summary: Iteration 45: Admin Integration Settings Panel Testing. All 9 backend tests PASSED (100%). Frontend testing confirmed Integrations tab works correctly with Stripe showing 'CONFIGURED', PayPal showing 'NOT SET', Resend showing 'CONFIGURED'. Save button works with success toast.
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/admin/integrations correctly returns masked keys with status (has_key, enabled) for each provider
PUT /api/admin/integrations correctly updates settings and persists to MongoDB system_config collection
Non-admin users correctly receive 403 Forbidden when accessing integrations endpoints
IntegrationsTab.jsx correctly displays Stripe, PayPal, and Resend cards with proper status indicators
PayPal mode toggle (sandbox/live) is functional and persists correctly
Keys are properly masked (showing only last 4 characters) for security

success_rate.frontend: 100% - Integrations tab displays correctly with all status indicators

seed_data_creation: None - used existing test data and .env configured keys
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin Integrations Settings panel is fully functional. To access: login as admin -> click 'Integrations' tab. Stripe and Resend are configured via .env (STRIPE_API_KEY, RESEND_API_KEY). PayPal keys are NOT configured (PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID, PAYPAL_SECRET are empty in .env). Keys stored in MongoDB system_config collection with key 'integration_keys' will override .env values.

frontend_verification.integrations_tab_visible: PASSED - Tab shows in admin nav with key icon
frontend_verification.stripe_card: PASSED - Shows 'CONFIGURED' status with masked API key
frontend_verification.paypal_card: PASSED - Shows 'NOT SET' status with sandbox/live toggle
frontend_verification.resend_card: PASSED - Shows 'CONFIGURED' status with sender email field
frontend_verification.save_button: PASSED - Click shows 'Integration settings saved!' toast
frontend_verification.enable_toggles: PASSED - Stripe enabled, PayPal disabled, Resend enabled

mocked_apis.note: All APIs are real implementations. Integration keys come from .env with DB override capability.
